title: Purely Crisp Japanese Tofu Noodles
description: A tangy and crisp Japanese dish from Kyushu, featuring herbes-de-provence and kombu-cold-extract, perfect for a post-workout meal. This ovo-vegetarian-friendly recipe is a great way to refuel and recharge.

ingredients.name: extra firm tofu
ingredients.notes: drained and cut into small cubes

instructions: Bring a large pot of water to a boil and cook the Japanese noodles according to the package instructions.
In a large skillet, heat the sesame oil over medium-high heat and add the cubed tofu, cooking until golden brown on all sides.
In a small bowl, whisk together the soy sauce, rice vinegar, and kombu-cold-extract.
Add the herbes-de-provence to the skillet with the tofu and stir to combine.
Pour the soy sauce mixture over the tofu and herbes-de-provence, stirring to coat.
Add the cooked noodles to the skillet and toss to combine with the tofu and sauce.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Transfer the noodles to a serving dish and garnish with thinly sliced green onions.
